title: We deployed AI to 1M users. What we learned the hard way
post:
We deployed an AI feature to 1 million users last quarter. Three things we didn't anticipate:

1. Performance at scale doesn't match performance in testing
Our model handled 100 users perfectly. At 1M, latency spiked, hallucinations increased 3x, and edge cases exploded.

2. Users find creative ways to break AI
They do this on purpose. Every 'jailbreak' technique taught us more about our failure modes than a year of internal testing.

3. The monitoring problem is unsolved
We had dashboards for server metrics. We had nothing for 'is the AI actually doing what we want?'

The biggest lesson: production AI requires a completely different skill set than training AI. The engineers who build it and the engineers who run it cannot be the same people.

What deployment lessons have you learned?
